_note: Acoustic-anchor SFX cue taxonomy (implements wiki concepts/sfx-cue-taxonomy-2026-06). Read by scripts/lib/sfx_cues.py at Stage 7 (profile-mode) to place sound effects on the moment's beats instead of the vision/zoom-tied guess. Gated by CLIP_SFX_ANCHOR (default on); CLIP_SFX_ANCHOR=0 reverts to the legacy profile synthesis. Failure-soft: a missing/unreadable file falls back to the in-code defaults in sfx_cues.py. CONFIDENCE: beat->sound mappings are practitioner convention (cross-source, not formally proven) and licensing-only claims were the verified ones in the research; treat offsets/gains as starting defaults to A/B-test.
_density_note: R4 apply 2026-07-11 (corpus_diff report #1, owner-approved): reference clips carry far denser sound furniture than ours (we averaged ~1 cue/clip). max_cues 4->6, buildup risers on funny/reactive, and secondary_peaks drops ducked punchline_light hits on the clip's strongest OTHER acoustic transients (laughter bursts, exclamations, slams) - the roast-cadence mechanism the owner asked for ('there could have been visual and sound effects after each diss').
_dedup_note: Min gap between cues (s). Kept BELOW the build-up lead (1.0s) so a riser and its payoff hit coexist as a one-two punch; large enough to merge genuinely coincident cues (e.g. two laughter markers).
_beat_defaults_note: Per beat-type: an ORDERED list of sound options. The builder emits the FIRST kind whose assets/sfx/<kind>/ folder has audio (sfx_inject.has_assets), so the feature works today with the seeded whoosh/impact/ding/riser/boom libraries and automatically upgrades to the ideal kind (scratch, sad_trombone, applause, crickets, bruh, pop) once those are seeded. offset_s is added to the beat time (negative = before). gain_db is relative to the source audio (0 = at speech level, negative = ducked under speech); linear = 10^(gain_db/20).

beat_defaults._buildup_note: offset_s is 0.0 here: the build-up anchor is already placed buildup_lead_s before the payoff in sfx_cues.py, so the riser must NOT subtract a second lead (that double-penalty put it ~2s early, outside the research's 0.4-1.25s window).

_category_beats_note: Maps the moment's primary_category to which beat the payoff is + which supplementary beats to scan for. payoff=null means no SFX for that category (emotional clips stay clean, matching style_profiles' empty SFX pools). scan_laughter adds a punchline beat at each transcript laughter marker inside the clip; buildup adds a riser before the payoff.
_timing_note: Owner feedback 2026-07-04 ('effects came in a little too early'): the moment timestamp is DETECTION time, a beat before the actual punchline. payoff_delay_s shifts the payoff anchor later; snap_to_onset refines it to the strongest acoustic transient within onset_snap_window_s after it (the real hit), falling back to the fixed delay. The riser keeps its buildup_lead_s lead relative to the REFINED payoff. Adaptive GAIN lives in env, not here: CLIP_SFX_ADAPTIVE=0 kills it, CLIP_SFX_REF_DB (default -20), CLIP_SFX_ADAPT_MAX_DB (default 9).
_timing2_note: Owner findings 2026-07-05: payoff_rescue — when the detected payoff sits in the first max(3s,12%) of a >12s clip, the timestamp is the SETUP not the payoff (Hot Cheeto: boom@1s, real beat@18s) -> search the whole clip for the dominant transient. boom_after_line — a 0dB boom ON the punchline masks the words (Shower Bluff) -> shift into the first speech gap (RMS dip <35% of peak, >=150ms) within boom_gap_window_s after the hit: line -> breath -> boom. Floor: no payoff cue in the first 2.5s of clips >8s.
_global_mix_note: RESERVED / informational. The ACTIVE per-cue mix policy is the gain_db on each beat_defaults entry (applied by sfx_inject._cue_volume): boom rides hot at gain_db 0.0 (the research's 'loud = funny' Vine-boom exception) while every other kind ducks under speech (negative dB). duck_speech_db and hot_kinds below are not read by code today — kept for a future calibration loop that may fit the mix globally. loudnorm_target_lufs is likewise deferred (not applied — pumping/perf risk on the existing audio chain).
_kind_sources_note: Which assets/sfx/<kind>/ folders back each semantic kind, and which still need CC0 seeding. boom is aliased to the impact library via assets/sfx/boom/library.json so punchline SFX work immediately. The rest fall through the beat_defaults priority list to a seeded kind until their own CC0 assets are added (Freesound CC0 / ZapSplat CC0 per the research; exclude CC-BY-NC for monetized channels).
